taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete statements about the cardiovascular system.
textField:
The three layers of a blood vessel wall are tunica *intima*, tunica *media*, and tunica *adventitia*.

Elastic arteries exhibit the *Windkessel* function — they stretch during systole and *recoil* during diastole to maintain continuous blood flow.

Arterioles are called *resistance* vessels because they are the main determinants of peripheral vascular resistance.

An *end* artery has no anastomoses, so its blockage leads to *infarction* of the tissue it supplies.

In the fetal circulation, the *ductus arteriosus* shunts blood from the pulmonary trunk to the aorta, bypassing the non-functional *lungs*.

Capillaries are called *exchange* vessels because gas and nutrient exchange occurs across their thin walls.

Veins contain *valves* to prevent backflow of blood and are called *capacitance* vessels as they hold approximately 70% of blood volume.

The *vasa vasorum* are small blood vessels that supply the walls of large arteries and veins.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review cardiovascular system features and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the cardiovascular system!
